implication: Two colors within a CIE delta-E of 5.0 are perceptually the same to the embroidery pipeline and may be merged into one layer. A tracer that separates colors finer than this budget produces redundant near-duplicate layers that add nodes without adding visible fidelity; a tracer that merges too aggressively drops real colors.

param_mapping.rationale: vtracer's layer_difference is the color-distance gate for spawning a new stacked layer (analogous to delta-E merge tolerance). Lower values split more colors, higher values merge. The doctrine's delta_e<=5.0 argues for a moderate value (~8-16) rather than the very-fine 4, so near-identical colors collapse into one layer. Note: layer_difference is a vtracer RGB-ish distance, NOT a calibrated CIE delta-E, so this is a conceptual (inferred) mapping, not a direct unit match.

hypothesis: Setting layer_difference near the middle of its ladder (8-16) rather than the minimum (4) will improve color_fidelity-per-layer (fewer redundant near-duplicate layers) without measurably lowering ssim, because merges below delta_e~5 are perceptually invisible.
